_meta.note: Cowork batch (10 sub-agents par sous-genre/décennie). Exclut les 20 du seed Phase 13.0. Merge dans artists.js à valider par Sébastien.

buddy_guy.name: Buddy Guy

guitars: Fender Buddy Guy Polka Dot Stratocaster
pedals: Dunlop BG95 Buddy Guy Cry Baby Wah

buddy_guy.notes: Chicago blues icon whose cranked Fender Bassman and Strat (later the polka-dot signature) defined a raw, dynamic tone driven from the hands.

albert_king.name: Albert King
albert_king.notes: Left-handed bluesman who played a flipped, upside-down Gibson Flying V tuned to an open dropped tuning, fingerstyle, with string-bending that shaped modern blues lead.

freddie_king.name: Freddie King
freddie_king.notes: Texas-Chicago blues great who moved from a P-90 goldtop Les Paul to his trademark cherry Gibson ES-345 cranked through loud Fender amps with treble maxed.

muddy_waters.name: Muddy Waters
muddy_waters.notes: Father of modern Chicago blues; played a blonde 1957-58 Telecaster nicknamed 'The Hoss' (later refretted with rosewood) through Fender amps with slide and a deep, declamatory tone.

hubert_sumlin.name: Hubert Sumlin
hubert_sumlin.notes: Howlin' Wolf's lead guitarist and a foundational Chicago blues stylist; his fingerpicked, jagged single-note lines on a '50s Gibson Les Paul shaped the Wolf's classic sides.

dick_dale.name: Dick Dale
dick_dale.notes: The 'King of the Surf Guitar'; his heavy-gauge, left-strung gold Strat 'The Beast' through the Fender Dual Showman (which he co-developed with Leo Fender) and reverb tank defined surf tone.

duane_eddy.name: Duane Eddy
duane_eddy.notes: Instrumental-rock pioneer whose low-string 'twang' on a Gretsch 6120 with Bigsby, run through a hot-rodded 100-watt Magnatone soaked in reverb and tremolo, sold millions.

link_wray.name: Link Wray
bands: Link Wray and His Ray Men
link_wray.notes: Pioneer of the power chord and distortion; his 1958 instrumental 'Rumble' was cut through a Premier amp with the speakers punctured by pencil holes to force raw breakup.

steve_cropper.name: Steve Cropper
steve_cropper.notes: Stax house guitarist and Booker T. & the M.G.'s member; his rosewood-neck Telecaster and small Fender amps gave the clean, biting rhythm sound on countless soul classics.

curtis_mayfield.name: Curtis Mayfield
curtis_mayfield.notes: Soul innovator who tuned to an open F-sharp (F#-A#-C#-F#-A#-F#, learned from the piano's black keys) and played thumb-and-fingers through a Fender Twin for his signature shimmer.

otis_rush.name: Otis Rush
otis_rush.notes: West Side Chicago blues master and left-handed player (guitar strung upside-down) whose intense vibrato and minor-key phrasing on a Strat, later semi-hollow Gibsons, deeply influenced blues-rock.

roy_buchanan.name: Roy Buchanan
roy_buchanan.notes: Telecaster virtuoso who played his 1953 Tele 'Nancy' through a Fender Vibrolux dimed, controlling tone entirely from the guitar's volume/tone for pinch harmonics and volume-swell vocal effects.

james_jamerson.name: James Jamerson
james_jamerson.notes: Motown's uncredited genius behind hundreds of hits; his 1962 Precision 'Funk Machine' with La Bella flatwounds, foam mute and an Ampeg B-15 created the melodic, syncopated Motown bass sound.

duck_dunn.name: Donald "Duck" Dunn
duck_dunn.notes: Stax house bassist and Booker T. & the M.G.'s member; his 1959 Precision with La Bella flatwounds through an Ampeg B-15 anchored the deep, in-the-pocket Memphis soul groove.

carol_kaye.name: Carol Kaye
carol_kaye.notes: Prolific Wrecking Crew session bassist on thousands of 60s hits; her Fender Precision with heavy La Bella flatwounds, high action and a pick gave the punchy, articulate L.A. studio bass sound.

bill_wyman.name: Bill Wyman
bill_wyman.notes: Rolling Stones founding bassist; played a hollowbody Framus Star live and a cheap Dallas Tuxedo in the studio, later a Fender Mustang, with a Vox AC30 that helped land him the gig.

paul_mccartney.name: Paul McCartney
paul_mccartney.notes: The Beatles' melodic bassist; his light Hofner 500/1 violin bass defined the early years, then the Rickenbacker 4001S brought a fuller tone on Sgt. Pepper, the White Album and Abbey Road.

bill_black.name: Bill Black
bill_black.notes: Elvis Presley's original bassist; his slap upright bass drove the Sun rockabilly recordings, and he became one of the first to use the Fender Precision Bass in popular music on 'Jailhouse Rock'.

noel_redding.name: Noel Redding
noel_redding.notes: Bassist of The Jimi Hendrix Experience; a converted guitarist, he played a 1966 Fender Jazz Bass with a pick and a trebly, distorted tone through overdriven Sunn and Marshall stacks.

larry_graham.name: Larry Graham
larry_graham.notes: Inventor of the slap-and-pop ('thumpin' and pluckin'') bass technique with Sly and the Family Stone; his punchy Fender Jazz through an Acoustic 360 became the template for funk bass.

jack_casady.name: Jack Casady
jack_casady.notes: Jefferson Airplane and Hot Tuna bassist whose modified Guild Starfire (re-circuited by Owsley Stanley) through an overdriven Versatone gave a singing, melodic psychedelic bass voice.

pete_townshend.name: Pete Townshend
pete_townshend.notes: Best known for the Gibson SG with P-90s into Hiwatt stacks live (1968-73); studio classics used a Gretsch 6120 into a Fender Bandmaster. Early Who era was Rickenbacker into Vox/Marshall.

jeff_beck.name: Jeff Beck
jeff_beck.notes: Yardbirds era was a '54 Esquire into Vox AC30; the Truth/Beck-Ola hard rock tone was a '59 Les Paul (and stripped Strat) into Marshall. Settled into Fender signature Strats into Marshall + Vibro-King from the 90s.

george_harrison.name: George Harrison
george_harrison.notes: Defined the British Invasion jangle with the Rickenbacker 360/12 and Gretsch hollowbodies into Vox AC30 Top Boost. Mid-60s onward added the Epiphone Casino and a Sonic Blue Strat ('Rocky').

keith_richards.name: Keith Richards
keith_richards.notes: Signature sound is a 5-string open-G '53 Telecaster ('Micawber') into a tweed Fender Twin. 60s Stones work leaned on a Les Paul and other Gibsons.

dave_davies.name: Dave Davies
dave_davies.notes: Pioneered rock distortion on 'You Really Got Me' by slashing the speaker of a small green Elpico amp run into a Vox AC30. Became known for the 1958 Gibson Flying V from the late 60s through the 70s.

roger_mcguinn.name: Roger McGuinn
roger_mcguinn.notes: The chiming 12-string jangle of the Byrds came from a Rickenbacker 360/12 (later modified to a 370/12 with built-in compressor) into Vox AC30, inspired by George Harrison.

hank_marvin.name: Hank Marvin
hank_marvin.notes: British guitar pioneer whose clean, echo-drenched Stratocaster (the first Strat in the UK) into Vox AC15/AC30 with tape/drum echo defined the instrumental sound of The Shadows.

peter_green.name: Peter Green
peter_green.notes: Blues tone built on his out-of-phase 1959 Les Paul 'Greeny' (later sold to Gary Moore) into Orange Matamp and silverface Fender Twin/Deluxe Reverb during the early Fleetwood Mac years.

mick_taylor.name: Mick Taylor
mick_taylor.notes: His fluid Stones-era tone (Sticky Fingers, Exile) was a late-50s sunburst Les Paul bought from Keith Richards into Ampeg SVT live, plus a Gibson SG for slide; later moved to Marshall.

robbie_robertson.name: Robbie Robertson
robbie_robertson.notes: Backed Dylan and recorded early Band albums on a 1965 Telecaster; from the early 70s switched to his famous bronzed '54 Stratocaster, both into a tweed-era Fender Twin.

jorma_kaukonen.name: Jorma Kaukonen
jorma_kaukonen.notes: Psychedelic fingerstyle leads on Surrealistic Pillow used a Guild Thunderbird into a Standel; his signature Airplane tone was a Gibson ES-345 Stereo into multiple Fender Twin Reverbs with an Ampeg Scrambler and wah.

jerry_garcia.name: Jerry Garcia
jerry_garcia.notes: Long-running tone centered on heavily modified Fender Twin Reverb preamps driving McIntosh power amps into JBL-loaded cabs, paired with custom Doug Irwin guitars Wolf (1973-79) and Tiger (1979-95).

carlos_santana.name: Carlos Santana
carlos_santana.notes: Singing, sustained lead tone built on a Gibson SG Special at Woodstock, then the Yamaha SG2000 and signature PRS, famously paired with Mesa/Boogie amps (which he helped launch) and later Dumble.

frank_zappa.name: Frank Zappa
frank_zappa.notes: Mid-70s lead tone centered on a heavily modified black Gibson SG into a 100-watt Marshall JMP plus an Acoustic 270; 80s rig added modified Stratocasters and a switching system blending Marshall, Vox and Mesa.

alvin_lee.name: Alvin Lee
alvin_lee.notes: Lightning-fast Woodstock blues-rock on his cherry Gibson ES-335 'Big Red' run straight into cranked Marshall heads, no pedals; used the guitar's volume to clean up.

leslie_west.name: Leslie West
leslie_west.notes: Massive 'Mississippi Queen' tone from a single-pickup Gibson Les Paul Junior into a Sunn Coliseum PA head with 4x12 cabs; later used Marshall JMP in the studio and JCM900 live.

tony_hicks.name: Tony Hicks
tony_hicks.notes: Clean, intricate British Invasion parts under the Hollies' harmonies, played on a cherry/black Gibson ES-345 and Vox Phantom XII through Vox AC30/AC50 amps.

ron_wood.name: Ron Wood
ron_wood.notes: Raw Faces tone came from a Gibson SG / Zemaitis into Ampeg SVT; with the Stones his go-to is a '55 sunburst Strat (and '52 Tele) cranked through tweed Fender Twins and a Vibro-King.

steve_marriott.name: Steve Marriott
steve_marriott.notes: Roaring Humble Pie tone from a single-pickup Epiphone Coronet (and later a black Les Paul Custom) strung heavy into a stacked Marshall rig; Small Faces era used Gretsch, Tele and Les Paul.

terry_kath.name: Terry Kath
terry_kath.notes: Chicago's fiery guitarist (praised by Hendrix); early work on a Strat and Gibsons into Fender Dual Showman/Acoustic amps, then settled on a humbucker-loaded blonde Telecaster from 1972 on.

joe_perry.name: Joe Perry
joe_perry.notes: Played Les Pauls live throughout the '70s but recorded much of Aerosmith's early material on Strats; used a Les Paul Junior with P-90 for 'Walk This Way'.

brad_whitford.name: Brad Whitford
brad_whitford.notes: Aerosmith's rhythm/lead foil to Joe Perry; classic '70s rig was a Gibson Les Paul into a Marshall Super Lead and 4x12.

scott_gorham.name: Scott Gorham
scott_gorham.notes: Half of Thin Lizzy's twin-lead guitar partnership; used Les Paul Deluxes (mini-humbuckers) early, switching to a '57 Standard after Jailbreak, into EL-34 Marshalls.

brian_robertson.name: Brian Robertson
brian_robertson.notes: Thin Lizzy's other twin-lead guitarist; Les Paul with PAF pickups into early-'70s non-master-volume 100W Marshalls, famous for expressive wah solos.

michael_schenker.name: Michael Schenker
michael_schenker.notes: His UFO-era Gibson Flying V into a 50-watt Marshall with a wah became one of rock's most imitated tones.

rudolf_schenker.name: Rudolf Schenker
rudolf_schenker.notes: Scorpions' rhythm-guitar engine; signature Gibson Flying V into Marshall JMP heads, with a Korina '58 V among his most prized.

uli_jon_roth.name: Uli Jon Roth
uli_jon_roth.notes: Hendrix-inspired Scorpions lead guitarist; a 1972 100W Marshall Super Lead Tremolo paired with maple-board CBS Strats, plus Fender Showmans for clean rhythm.

alex_lifeson.name: Alex Lifeson
alex_lifeson.notes: Rush's guitarist; early ES-335 then white ES-355, running Marshall heads alongside a Hiwatt Custom 100 for both crunch and clarity.

steve_howe.name: Steve Howe
steve_howe.notes: Yes's eclectic lead guitarist; defined the band's early tone with a Gibson ES-175 into a Fender Dual Showman, later moving to Twins for live projection.

steve_hackett.name: Steve Hackett
steve_hackett.notes: Genesis's pioneering prog guitarist; his 1957 Les Paul Goldtop through Hiwatt amps (with small amps for studio) defined solos like 'Firth of Fifth'.

robert_fripp.name: Robert Fripp
robert_fripp.notes: King Crimson's founder; a three-pickup Les Paul Custom 'Black Beauty' into Hiwatt DR103 (and Marshall) heads, famous for sustain, fuzz and controlled feedback.

martin_barre.name: Martin Barre
martin_barre.notes: Jethro Tull's guitarist; recorded 'Aqualung' with a '58 Les Paul Junior into a Hiwatt 4x12, switching to Marshalls around 1975.

tom_scholz.name: Tom Scholz
tom_scholz.notes: Boston's mastermind; recorded the debut with a modified '68 Les Paul Deluxe ('Mighty Mouse') into a Marshall Super Lead with a homemade attenuator. (Rockman came later, c.1982.)

ace_frehley.name: Ace Frehley
ace_frehley.notes: KISS's lead guitarist; '73 Les Paul Deluxe modded with DiMarzio Super Distortion pickups into Marshall Super Leads — 'plug a Les Paul into a Marshall, everything on 10'.

paul_stanley.name: Paul Stanley
paul_stanley.notes: KISS's rhythm guitarist; took up the Ibanez Iceman from 1977 and is a self-described 'guitar-and-Marshall man', having improvised modified Fender amps before affording Marshalls.

phil_manzanera.name: Phil Manzanera
phil_manzanera.notes: Roxy Music's guitarist; defined by his 'Cardinal Red' 1964 Gibson Firebird VII into a Hiwatt with a WEM cabinet and a Fender Twin.

andy_powell.name: Andy Powell
andy_powell.notes: Wishbone Ash's guitarist and twin-lead pioneer; his circa-1967 Gibson Flying V into Orange amps (a deal via manager Miles Copeland) became iconic, with tweed Fenders in the studio.

ted_nugent.name: Ted Nugent
ted_nugent.notes: Famous for his feedback-prone Gibson Byrdland archtop driven loud through walls of Fender Twin Reverb and Dual Showman amps; recorded 'Cat Scratch Fever' on a '62 Byrdland and a '62 Fender Deluxe.

frank_marino.name: Frank Marino
frank_marino.notes: Hendrix-inspired Mahogany Rush guitarist; favored '61 Gibson SGs and, unusually, a solid-state Acoustic 270 for live work in the '70s, with Marshalls in the studio.

pat_travers.name: Pat Travers
pat_travers.notes: Canadian hard-rock guitarist ('Boom Boom (Out Go the Lights)'); ran 50W and 100W Marshall Super Leads into Greenback 4x12s for his signature crunch.

robin_trower.name: Robin Trower
robin_trower.notes: Hendrix-influenced power-trio guitarist; a 1973 maple-neck Strat into a Marshall Super Lead with a Uni-Vibe defined 'Bridge of Sighs'.

nancy_wilson.name: Nancy Wilson
nancy_wilson.notes: Heart's guitarist; known for driving acoustic rhythm on Ovation electro-acoustics (chosen to fight feedback live) and Les Pauls for electric work in Heart's early hits.

duane_allman.name: Duane Allman
duane_allman.notes: Slide guitar pioneer; cherry sunburst '59 Les Paul through a 50W Marshall defined the Fillmore East sound. Minimal effects.

dickey_betts.name: Dickey Betts
dickey_betts.notes: Favored a cleaner 100W Marshall vs Duane's 50W, giving more headroom for his melodic dual-lead harmonies.

billy_gibbons.name: Billy Gibbons
billy_gibbons.notes: 'Pearly Gates' '59 Les Paul into a '68 100W Marshall Super Lead is the foundational ZZ Top tone. Uses ultra-light .007-.038 strings.

gary_rossington.name: Gary Rossington
gary_rossington.notes: Les Paul into a non-master-volume Marshall Super Lead defined his big, simple tone on the first three Skynyrd albums.

allen_collins.name: Allen Collins
allen_collins.notes: Played a '58 korina Explorer on 'Free Bird' and a P-90 Firebird through Fender and Marshall amps; the fiery lead foil to Rossington.

john_mclaughlin.name: John McLaughlin
john_mclaughlin.notes: Gibson EDS-1275 doubleneck through a cranked 100W Marshall defined the trailblazing jazz-fusion tone of early Mahavishnu Orchestra.

al_di_meola.name: Al Di Meola

guitars: Gibson Les Paul Custom '71 (black, DiMarzio pickups)

al_di_meola.notes: His '71 black Les Paul Custom through a 50W Marshall and 4x12 cab cut the articulate fusion tone on Return to Forever and Elegant Gypsy.

larry_carlton.name: Larry Carlton

pedals: TC Electronic Hall of Fame Reverb

larry_carlton.notes: 'Mr. 335' — his '69 ES-335 straight into a tweed Fender Deluxe shaped his Steely Dan and Crusaders session tone.

nile_rodgers.name: Nile Rodgers

guitars: Fender Stratocaster '60 (The Hitmaker, Olympic White, '59 neck)

nile_rodgers.notes: The 'Hitmaker' Strat (neck pickup, picked near the neck) into a clean Deluxe Reverb — or direct to the desk — defined funk-disco rhythm guitar.

mick_ronson.name: Mick Ronson
mick_ronson.notes: Stripped '68 Les Paul Custom into a 200W Marshall Major with a cocked wah and Tone Bender — the glam-rock lead sound of Ziggy Stardust.

marc_bolan.name: Marc Bolan

guitars: Gibson Les Paul Standard (late '50s, refinished 'Bolan Chablis' orange)

marc_bolan.notes: Orange-refinished '50s Les Paul through a Vampower stack with a Rangemaster treble booster gave the gritty glam-rock T. Rex tone.

johnny_ramone.name: Johnny Ramone

guitars: Mosrite Ventures II (white, DiMarzio Super Distortion)

johnny_ramone.notes: White Mosrite Ventures II straight into cranked Marshalls, all downstrokes, zero effects — the template for punk rhythm guitar.

mick_jones.name: Mick Jones
mick_jones.notes: Early Clash: Les Paul Junior into an Ampeg V4. By London Calling: a stable of Les Pauls into Mesa/Boogie combos with Roland echo units.

steve_jones.name: Steve Jones
steve_jones.notes: White '74 Les Paul Custom through a cranked silverface Twin Reverb — the layered wall-of-guitars on Never Mind the Bollocks.

eddie_hazel.name: Eddie Hazel
eddie_hazel.notes: Strat into a fuzz box, wah and dub-style delay produced the legendary 'Maggot Brain' solo for Funkadelic.

ernie_isley.name: Ernie Isley
ernie_isley.notes: '71 black Strat into a Twin Reverb with a Big Muff and Maestro phaser cut the iconic fuzz lead on 'That Lady'.

geddy_lee.name: Geddy Lee
geddy_lee.notes: The Rickenbacker 4001 through Ampeg SVTs defined his bright, aggressive 70s tone; later switched to a vintage Jazz Bass and Orange AD200 heads.

chris_squire.name: Chris Squire
chris_squire.notes: His shaved-down '64 Rickenbacker (RM1999) with Rotosound roundwounds into treble-heavy Marshall gave the bright, grinding bass tone that cut through Yes arrangements.

john_entwistle.name: John Entwistle
john_entwistle.notes: The 'Frankenstein' Precision through Hiwatt stacks powered Tommy, Live at Leeds and Who's Next; later moved to Gibson Thunderbirds and Alembics.

bootsy_collins.name: Bootsy Collins

guitars: Fender Precision Bass '75 (custom finish, J pickup at bridge)

bootsy_collins.notes: His Ampeg SVT rig with a Mu-Tron III envelope filter created the psychedelic, squelchy 'space funk' bass for James Brown and P-Funk.

stanley_clarke.name: Stanley Clarke
stanley_clarke.notes: Custom short-scale Alembic basses through Ampeg gave the bright, articulate lead-bass tone that defined jazz fusion with Return to Forever.

jaco_pastorius.name: Jaco Pastorius

guitars: Fender Jazz Bass '62 fretless (Bass of Doom)

jaco_pastorius.notes: The fretless '62 'Bass of Doom' with roundwounds through Acoustic 360 amps (midrange-boosted Variamp) created the singing, growling fretless tone of Weather Report.

verdine_white.name: Verdine White
verdine_white.notes: Started on Fender Jazz and Precision basses recorded through an Ampeg B-15 for the punchy EWF funk-soul bass; later a longtime Sadowsky artist.

berry_oakley.name: Berry Oakley

guitars: Fender Jazz Bass '62 (The Tractor, Hagstrom Bi-Sonic pickup, 3 pickups)

berry_oakley.notes: His modified '62 Jazz Bass 'The Tractor' (Hagstrom Bi-Sonic plus two Jazz pickups, often split to two amps) anchored the Allman Brothers' Fillmore-era sound.

phil_lynott.name: Phil Lynott

guitars: Fender Precision Bass (black, maple, mirror pickguard, Badass bridge)

phil_lynott.notes: Bassist and lead vocalist of Thin Lizzy. His black mirror-pickguard Precision Bass with Rotosound strings through Hiwatt amps defined the band's driving rock sound.

roger_waters.name: Roger Waters
roger_waters.notes: Black Fender Precision through Hiwatt/WEM rigs underpinned The Dark Side of the Moon and The Wall; later moved to Ampeg SVT for tours.

glenn_hughes.name: Glenn Hughes
glenn_hughes.notes: Bassist and vocalist for Deep Purple's Mk III/IV. Recorded Burn on a '73 Rickenbacker 4001 (later given to Geezer Butler), then switched to a '63 Precision; signature Orange tone.

eddie_van_halen.name: Eddie Van Halen
eddie_van_halen.notes: Pioneered the 'brown sound' by dialing a 1968 Marshall Plexi down with a Variac. Switched to Peavey 5150 in 1992 and his own EVH 5150 III in the 2000s.

randy_rhoads.name: Randy Rhoads
randy_rhoads.notes: Defined early-80s metal lead tone with a Variac-starved 100W Marshall Plexi, his polka-dot Flying V and a 1974 Les Paul Custom on Blizzard of Ozz.

steve_vai.name: Steve Vai
steve_vai.notes: Designed the Ibanez JEM superstrat in 1985; ran large Marshall/Bogner rack rigs in the David Lee Roth and Whitesnake years before adopting Carvin Legacy amps from 1999.

joe_satriani.name: Joe Satriani
joe_satriani.notes: Recorded Surfing With The Alien on a modded '68 Marshall half-stack; endorsed Peavey JSX (2004-2010) before switching to his signature Marshall JVM410HJS in 2010.

yngwie_malmsteen.name: Yngwie Malmsteen

amps: Marshall JMP Mark II Super Lead 50W

yngwie_malmsteen.notes: Neoclassical pioneer; scalloped-fretboard '72 Strat 'The Duck' into walls of vintage Marshall JMP Mark II 50W Super Leads, later his signature Marshall YJM100.

paul_gilbert.name: Paul Gilbert
paul_gilbert.notes: Racer X/Mr. Big shredder; ran Laney heads and ADA preamps in the 80s before switching to Marshall (JCM2000 DSL, Vintage Modern) in the 90s for warmer radio-friendly tone.

george_lynch.name: George Lynch
george_lynch.notes: Dokken-era tone from modified Marshall Super Lead Plexis run at high voltage with Charvel superstrats; adopted ESP guitars (1986) and Soldano SLO-100 (1989) for Lynch Mob.

slash.name: Slash
slash.notes: Recorded Appetite for Destruction on a modded Marshall Super Lead with a Kris Derrig '59 Les Paul replica; the JCM800 2203 and Silver Jubilee define his live and studio tone since the GN'R era.

nuno_bettencourt.name: Nuno Bettencourt
nuno_bettencourt.notes: Funk-metal virtuoso whose signature Washburn N4 paired with cranked Marshall heads and an always-on Pro Co RAT define Extreme's tone.

zakk_wylde.name: Zakk Wylde
zakk_wylde.notes: Joined Ozzy in 1987 with a bullseye 1981 Les Paul Custom; the Marshall JCM800 2203 has been his signature amp ever since ('if it ain't broke, don't fix it').

mick_mars.name: Mick Mars
mick_mars.notes: Recorded Mötley Crüe's early albums on a black '72 Les Paul Custom through cranked Marshall JCM800s, favoring natural amp distortion over pedals.

steve_stevens.name: Steve Stevens
steve_stevens.notes: Rebel Yell was tracked through two JCM800s and a '69 Plexi; his signature Friedman head later split a Plexi-voiced dirty channel with a Fender Twin-style clean.

jake_e_lee.name: Jake E. Lee
jake_e_lee.notes: Used stock Marshall JCM800 2203 heads live with Ozzy and Badlands, plus vintage Marshall Plexi and 50W heads in the studio, fronted by a white Charvel and a Boss OD-1.

richie_sambora.name: Richie Sambora
richie_sambora.notes: Bon Jovi's overdriven crunch during the peak era came from Marshall JCM800 heads paired with Kramer star-inlay guitars; later moved to JCM2000 DSL100 and Blackstar.

jason_becker.name: Jason Becker
jason_becker.notes: Neoclassical shredder; used an ADA MP-1 preamp and Carvin X-100B stack in Cacophony, then eight Marshalls for the David Lee Roth A Little Ain't Enough sessions.

vito_bratta.name: Vito Bratta
vito_bratta.notes: Hi-tech-minded White Lion virtuoso who paired a white EMG-loaded Steinberger with ADA MP-1 preamps and vintage Marshalls for the Pride and Big Game tones.

warren_demartini.name: Warren DeMartini
warren_demartini.notes: Ratt's lead tone came from Marshall JCM800 and mid-70s Plexi heads with rack delays, driven by his iconic snakeskin Charvel San Dimas superstrats.

reb_beach.name: Reb Beach

amps: Marshall JMP Master Volume Mark II
Marshall JMP Master Volume Mark II

reb_beach.notes: Winger's articulate shred tone came from Suhr-modded 1976 Marshall Master Volume Mark II heads and Ibanez RBM signatures, later joined Whitesnake on Suhr guitars.

john_sykes.name: John Sykes
john_sykes.notes: Tracked the Whitesnake '87 and Blue Murder albums on two Mesa Boogie Coliseum (Mark III) heads with his black '78 Les Paul Custom loaded with a Dirty Fingers bridge pickup.

tom_keifer.name: Tom Keifer
tom_keifer.notes: Cinderella's bluesy hard-rock tone came from Gibson Les Pauls into Marshall JCM800 and vintage Super Lead heads with old Greenback cabs.

tony_macalpine.name: Tony MacAlpine
tony_macalpine.notes: A driver of the 80s neoclassical shred boom; recorded early albums (Edge of Insanity, Maximum Security) on Peavey and Rockman gear with Kramer and B.C. Rich guitars.

vinnie_moore.name: Vinnie Moore
vinnie_moore.notes: Recorded the landmark Mind's Eye on a Marshall (JCM800 50W 2205) with an Ibanez and Super Distortion pickups; took on the Laney endorsement by Time Odyssey.

brad_gillis.name: Brad Gillis
brad_gillis.notes: Whammy-bar specialist who toured Ozzy and Night Ranger with a distressed '62 Strat and Marshall heads, later moving to Mesa Boogie and Soldano.

adrian_vandenberg.name: Adrian Vandenberg
adrian_vandenberg.notes: Led his own band Vandenberg and joined Whitesnake for the late-80s touring era; used modified Marshall heads and later Peavey VTM amps with Fernandes and Peavey signature superstrats.

greg_howe.name: Greg Howe
greg_howe.notes: Signed to Shrapnel in 1987; his fusion-shred tone leaned on Peavey HP Special amps (red-knob Dual Showman-style) and a TS808, later his DV Mark signature head.

izzy_stradlin.name: Izzy Stradlin
izzy_stradlin.notes: GN'R rhythm engine; tracked Appetite for Destruction with a '87 Gibson ES-175 and a sunburst Telecaster into a blackface Fender Bassman with a Mesa 4x12 (EV speakers).

glenn_tipton.name: Glenn Tipton
glenn_tipton.notes: Co-lead guitarist of Judas Priest, long associated with Marshall stacks (Super Lead then JCM800) and modified Hamer guitars before moving to ENGL in the late 2000s.

kk_downing.name: K.K. Downing
kk_downing.notes: Founding Judas Priest guitarist known for the Flying V shape (Gibson then Hamer) and Marshall JCM800 1959 Mk II Super Lead heads.

dave_murray.name: Dave Murray
dave_murray.notes: Iron Maiden's near-exclusive Stratocaster player (fitted with Seymour Duncan Hot Rails), mostly on Marshall, briefly Gallien-Krueger on Somewhere in Time and Seventh Son, later Axe-Fx.

adrian_smith.name: Adrian Smith
adrian_smith.notes: Iron Maiden guitarist with a more aggressive, riff-driven approach than Murray; long associated with his Jackson SC1 signature and Marshall amplification.

janick_gers.name: Janick Gers
janick_gers.notes: Joined Iron Maiden in 1990 as a third guitarist; a long-time Stratocaster proponent (Seymour Duncan JB Jr./Hot Rails) running Marshall JMP-1 preamps.

dave_mustaine.name: Dave Mustaine
dave_mustaine.notes: Megadeth founder; played a B.C. Rich Bich early on, then Jackson King V signatures with multiple Marshall JCM800s for the first Megadeth albums, before moving to ESP, Dean and modeling rigs.

marty_friedman.name: Marty Friedman
marty_friedman.notes: Lead guitarist on Megadeth's Rust In Peace and Countdown to Extinction era; main guitar was an early USA Jackson Kelly, recorded with Bogner and Soldano amps.

chris_poland.name: Chris Poland
chris_poland.notes: Lead guitarist on Megadeth's first two albums (Killing Is My Business, Peace Sells); used B.C. Rich guitars and a post-Plexi 1970s Marshall.

kerry_king.name: Kerry King
kerry_king.notes: Slayer co-guitarist and B.C. Rich loyalist; used a Mockingbird with DiMarzio Super Distortions on Reign In Blood through Marshall JCM800 2203 heads, later his signature B.C. Rich V with EMGs.

jeff_hanneman.name: Jeff Hanneman
jeff_hanneman.notes: Slayer guitarist and primary songwriter; used a modded Gibson Les Paul and B.C. Rich Mockingbird in the Reign In Blood era through Marshall JCM800 2203 heads, later moving to a signature Jackson Soloist with EMGs.

scott_ian.name: Scott Ian
scott_ian.notes: Anthrax rhythm guitarist and founder; a long-time Jackson artist who used a custom Rhoads and an '81 Gibson Flying V through '82 Marshall JCM800s in the Among the Living era, later Randall and EVH amps.

dan_spitz.name: Dan Spitz
dan_spitz.notes: Anthrax lead guitarist who designed custom Jackson Randy Rhoads guitars with Grover Jackson; switched from Marshall to modified Mesa Boogie Mark IIC heads around Among the Living.

alex_skolnick.name: Alex Skolnick
alex_skolnick.notes: Testament lead guitarist with a jazz-influenced approach; played Ibanez through Marshall in the 80s, later his ESP signature and Budda amps.

eric_peterson.name: Eric Peterson
eric_peterson.notes: Testament's rhythm guitarist and main riff writer; long associated with a black Les Paul fitted with EMG 81/85 pickups and his Dean signature.

gary_holt.name: Gary Holt
gary_holt.notes: Exodus guitarist and songwriter (also toured with Slayer); the classic Exodus tone is a Marshall JCM800, later running Silver Jubilee 2555X heads with his ESP signature loaded with EMGs.

andreas_kisser.name: Andreas Kisser
andreas_kisser.notes: Sepultura lead guitarist; a long-time Jackson Randy Rhoads player whose heaviest tone came from a Mesa Triaxis into a Mesa Strategy, later moving to Orange Rockerverb.

max_cavalera.name: Max Cavalera
max_cavalera.notes: Sepultura founder and rhythm guitarist (later Soulfly/Cavalera Conspiracy); known for his four-string detuned riffing and ESP signature guitars through Peavey 6505 amps.

mille_petrozza.name: Mille Petrozza
mille_petrozza.notes: Kreator frontman and guitarist; uses his ESP signature with a Diezel/ENGL-based tone (his signature ENGL Extreme Aggression amp), captured via Kemper for touring.

jeff_waters.name: Jeff Waters
jeff_waters.notes: Annihilator's founder, guitarist and producer; known for his technical thrash playing, later running Hughes & Kettner and EVH 5150III amps with his Epiphone signature V.

chuck_schuldiner.name: Chuck Schuldiner
chuck_schuldiner.notes: Founder of Death and a pioneer of death metal; played B.C. Rich Stealth guitars with a single DiMarzio X2N pickup through a famously simple Marshall Valvestate rig with minimal effects.

brian_tatler.name: Brian Tatler
brian_tatler.notes: Diamond Head founder and guitarist, a key NWOBHM songwriter (Am I Evil?, Helpless) heavily covered by Metallica; long associated with Gibson guitars and Marshall amplification.

phil_collen.name: Phil Collen
phil_collen.notes: Def Leppard guitarist who joined for Pyromania; used an Ibanez Destroyer and a 1978 Strat through Rockman processors on Hysteria, later developing the Jackson PC1 signature.

steve_clark.name: Steve Clark
steve_clark.notes: Def Leppard's co-founder and guitarist until his death in 1991; a dedicated Gibson Les Paul player who, like the band, recorded Hysteria largely through Rockman units rather than Marshall amps.

dimebag_darrell.name: Dimebag Darrell
dimebag_darrell.notes: Pantera and Damageplan guitarist; defined his tone with the lightning-bolt 'Dean From Hell' ML and solid-state Randall RG100ES heads, later collaborating with Krank on the Krankenstein.

the_edge.name: The Edge
the_edge.notes: Signature rhythmic delay-driven sound built on a 1976 Gibson Explorer, vintage Vox AC30 and dotted-eighth delays (Memory Man early, then the Korg SDD-3000 from the late 80s).

andy_summers.name: Andy Summers
andy_summers.notes: Defined The Police's clean, chorused tone with a modified 1963 Telecaster (onboard preamp + neck humbucker) through clean Marshall and Roland JC-120, with the Electric Mistress as his signature texture.

johnny_marr.name: Johnny Marr
johnny_marr.notes: Built The Smiths' jangle on a 1983 Rickenbacker 330 (and a Telecaster for many recordings) blended through a Roland JC-120 and a Fender Twin Reverb, topped with a Boss CE-2 chorus.

robert_smith.name: Robert Smith
robert_smith.notes: The Cure's gothic guitar sound centers on a Fender Jazzmaster through a Roland JC-120 in the early goth era, moving toward valve combos (Vox AC30, Orange) by Disintegration, with the Electric Mistress a constant.

peter_buck.name: Peter Buck
peter_buck.notes: R.E.M.'s jangle came from a Jetglo Rickenbacker 360 through Fender blackface amps; Buck used almost no pedals until around 1985, favoring amp tone with occasional chorus.

bernard_sumner.name: Bernard Sumner
bernard_sumner.notes: Recorded Joy Division's Unknown Pleasures on a Shergold Masquerader through a Vox UL730; with New Order moved to a Gibson ES-335 (bought in NY after gear theft) and live Vox AC30.

daniel_ash.name: Daniel Ash
daniel_ash.notes: Bauhaus and Love and Rockets' angular tone leaned on a Telecaster through HH solid-state and later Marshall amps, with a Boss DS-1, Copicat tape echo, chorus and an EBow shaping his textures.

will_sergeant.name: Will Sergeant
will_sergeant.notes: Echo and the Bunnymen's reverb- and delay-rich guitar relied on various Fenders into a Roland JC-120 and Fender Twin, with a Roland Space Echo and EBow central to his shimmering tone.

vernon_reid.name: Vernon Reid
vernon_reid.notes: Living Colour's eclectic metal-funk lead work used an EMG-loaded ESP Mirage and a Hamer custom ("the Trout") with Floyd Rose, through Marshall, plus pitch and wah effects.

john_frusciante.name: John Frusciante
john_frusciante.notes: Joined RHCP in late 1988 playing sticker-covered Ibanez guitars and a 1968 Stratocaster, with early rigs built on Marshall JCM800 and Mesa Boogie preamps (Mother's Milk era).

steve_harris.name: Steve Harris
steve_harris.notes: Iron Maiden's galloping bass comes from a mid-70s Fender Precision through Marshall cabs (EVM12L loaded) and a Trace Elliot preamp, with a fingerstyle attack and Rotosound strings.

cliff_burton.name: Cliff Burton

guitars: Aria Pro II SB Black 'n Gold

cliff_burton.notes: Metallica's lead-bass pioneer used a Rickenbacker 4001 (and Aria SB later) through Mesa Boogie D-180 heads with a Morley wah and distortion (Big Muff, then Tube Screamer) for his aggressive, melodic tone.

sting.name: Sting
sting.notes: The Police's bassist and frontman favored a 1962 Jazz Bass and a fretless/fretted Precision (and later Ibanez Musician) through Ampeg SVT stacks, often playing fingerstyle in a reggae-inflected style.

tony_levin.name: Tony Levin
tony_levin.notes: Prog and session bass icon who adopted the Music Man StingRay early and made the Chapman Stick central to King Crimson's Discipline; longtime Ampeg user with Peter Gabriel and King Crimson.

mark_king.name: Mark King
mark_king.notes: Level 42's slap-bass virtuoso built his sound on a Jaydee Supernatural (and Status Graphite) with very light Rotosound strings through Trace Elliot rigs, a defining tone of 80s British jazz-funk.

pino_palladino.name: Pino Palladino
pino_palladino.notes: Session bass great whose 80s signature was a fretless 1979 Music Man StingRay paired with a Boss OC-2 octave, heard on Paul Young's 'Wherever I Lay My Hat', adding melodic high-register lines.

billy_sheehan.name: Billy Sheehan
billy_sheehan.notes: Virtuoso rock bassist who played a heavily modified Precision ("The Wife") and Yamaha BB basses through dual Ampeg/Marshall rigs with split clean/dirty signals, later defining the Yamaha Attitude signature.

tina_weymouth.name: Tina Weymouth
tina_weymouth.notes: Talking Heads' bassist anchored the band's funky grooves with a hollowbody Hofner 500/2 Club bass and a red Fender Mustang, using flatwound strings and an auto-filter for live texture.

peter_hook.name: Peter Hook
peter_hook.notes: Pioneered high-register lead bass with Yamaha BB and a Shergold 6-string through Hiwatt (Joy Division) then an Alembic/Amcron rig (New Order), with a heavy chorus as his trademark.

adam_clayton.name: Adam Clayton
adam_clayton.notes: U2's bassist used Ibanez Musician basses into an Ampeg SVT 8x10 stack with no effects in the early 80s, later moving to vintage Fender Precision and Jazz basses for the Joshua Tree / Rattle and Hum era.

duff_mckagan.name: Duff McKagan
duff_mckagan.notes: Guns N' Roses' bassist recorded Appetite for Destruction on a Fender Jazz Bass Special (P-body, J-neck) through a Gallien-Krueger 800RB, with an Ibanez CS9 chorus giving his scooped, punchy tone its sheen.

mike_mills.name: Mike Mills
mike_mills.notes: R.E.M.'s melodic bassist used a Rickenbacker 4001 through Fender Dual Showman / Ampeg B-15 amps on the early albums (Murmur, Reckoning), moving to other basses and Mesa Boogie cabs by the late 80s.

kurt_cobain.name: Kurt Cobain
kurt_cobain.notes: Defined grunge tone with a left-handed Fender offset into a Boss DS-1 plus EHX Small Clone chorus; for Nevermind producer Butch Vig mixed Mesa Boogie, Fender Bassman and Vox AC30.

mike_mccready.name: Mike McCready
mike_mccready.notes: SRV-influenced Strat player with a 1960 Fender Stratocaster bought in 1991; dirty tone from cranked Marshalls, clean tone from a Fender Bassman/Twin.

stone_gossard.name: Stone Gossard
stone_gossard.notes: Pearl Jam's rhythm anchor, blending a Marshall JCM800 with a blackface Fender Twin via an Ernie Ball pan pedal; Les Paul Customs with Duncan Alnico II pickups.

chris_cornell.name: Chris Cornell
chris_cornell.notes: Soundgarden frontman and guitarist; studio tone on Superunknown blended a Marshall JMP 50 with a Mesa Dual Rectifier, with Gretsch Duo Jets and later a signature Gibson ES-335.

kim_thayil.name: Kim Thayil
kim_thayil.notes: Signature drop-tuned Soundgarden tone from a Guild S-100; used Peavey VTM-120 and Mesa Boogie amps on Badmotorfinger, later settling on Mesa Trem-O-Verb.

jerry_cantrell.name: Jerry Cantrell

pedals: MXR Jerry Cantrell Firefly Talk Box
Dunlop Jerry Cantrell Cry Baby Wah

jerry_cantrell.notes: Alice in Chains' dark layered tone built on a 1984 G&L Rampage and Bogner-modified Marshalls; signature talk box and Cry Baby wah on Man in the Box and Rooster.

billy_corgan.name: Billy Corgan
billy_corgan.notes: Wall-of-fuzz tone from a Fender Stratocaster ('Bat Strat') into a 1984 Marshall JCM800 (KT88 tubes) and a late-'70s op-amp EHX Big Muff across Gish, Siamese Dream and Mellon Collie.

james_iha.name: James Iha
james_iha.notes: Provided the Les Paul counterpart to Corgan's Strat in the Pumpkins; favored Gibson Les Paul Customs and Flying V into a Marshall JCM800, with a Fender Twin for cleans.

dave_grohl.name: Dave Grohl
dave_grohl.notes: Foo Fighters frontman whose signature guitar is a red Gibson Trini Lopez found in 1992; dirty tone from Mesa Boogie Road King heads, cleans from Vox AC30.

rivers_cuomo.name: Rivers Cuomo
rivers_cuomo.notes: Anti-effects approach on Weezer's Blue Album; recorded with a ~1977 Mesa Boogie Mark I through a Marshall cab using Gibson Les Paul Special/Junior and a borrowed Fender Jaguar rather than his iconic blue Strat.

jonny_greenwood.name: Jonny Greenwood
jonny_greenwood.notes: Radiohead's textural lead player on a Fender Telecaster Plus with Lace Sensors; dirty tone from a Marshall ShredMaster into a Fender solid-state, plus a Digitech Whammy on Paranoid Android.

ed_obrien.name: Ed O'Brien
ed_obrien.notes: Radiohead's atmospheric/textural guitarist; the Boss DD-5 delay defined the ambient sounds on The Bends and OK Computer, distorted tones from a Mesa Trem-O-Verb.

graham_coxon.name: Graham Coxon
graham_coxon.notes: Blur's inventive guitarist and self-described effects junkie; Fender Telecasters into Marshall Super Lead heads, with the ProCo Rat and a DOD Punkifier (used on Song 2) as signature dirt.

noel_gallagher.name: Noel Gallagher
noel_gallagher.notes: Oasis songwriter and lead guitarist; Definitely Maybe relied on a Marshall Valvestate/WEM combo with a Roland Space Echo, live tone from twin Marshall JCM900 stacks plus Epiphone Sheraton and Gibson semis.

dean_deleo.name: Dean DeLeo
dean_deleo.notes: Stone Temple Pilots' riff architect with a fleet of Gibson Les Pauls; layered wet/dry/wet rig built around Marshall 4x12s and a Vox AC30 for depth.

j_mascis.name: J Mascis
j_mascis.notes: Indie/grunge loud-guitar pioneer on a sunburst '63 Fender Jazzmaster into walls of Marshall Super Bass full-stacks and a Hiwatt DR-103, with EHX Big Muff as core fuzz.

doug_martsch.name: Doug Martsch
doug_martsch.notes: Built to Spill's layered-lead indie tone from a 1987 Fender Strat Plus (middle pickup only) into a custom 1968 Marshall 50W Plexi built by Jim Roth.

stephen_malkmus.name: Stephen Malkmus
stephen_malkmus.notes: Pavement's lo-fi indie guitarist; Slanted and Enchanted was essentially a Strat into a ProCo Rat through a small borrowed amp, later moving to Orange heads and a wider pedal palette.

frank_black.name: Frank Black
frank_black.notes: Pixies frontman/rhythm guitarist (Black Francis); keeps it single-coil simple with vintage Fender Teles into vintage Vox AC30 heads, plus a P-90 SG Junior for angrier tones.

joey_santiago.name: Joey Santiago
joey_santiago.notes: Pixies' lead guitarist; a Marshall JCM800 50W has been his dirty-tone mainstay on every album and tour, with a Fender Vibrolux Reverb for cleans and Les Paul Customs up front.

tom_morello.name: Tom Morello
tom_morello.notes: Famous for an effects-loop pedal setup and a single 50W Marshall JCM800 2205 used since 1988, paired with a Peavey 4x12 cab. Rig stayed essentially unchanged for three decades.

adam_jones.name: Adam Jones
adam_jones.notes: Layers multiple amps simultaneously — a 1976 Marshall Super Bass for warmth and a Diezel VH4 'blueface' for precision through Mesa 4x12 cabs, both consistent since 1994. Plays vintage Silverburst Les Paul Customs.

john_petrucci.name: John Petrucci

guitars: Ernie Ball Music Man JP signature

john_petrucci.notes: One of the world's foremost Mesa Mark IIC+ experts. Moved from Ibanez to his own Ernie Ball Music Man signatures, running dual Mesa Mark V heads in stereo with an Axe-Fx in the effects loop.

daron_malakian.name: Daron Malakian
daron_malakian.notes: Defined by his Ibanez Iceman and a no-dirt-pedal approach — all gain comes from the amp. Live he runs two generations of Friedman BE-100 into Marshall 4x12s, with only a Phase 90 and a delay on the board.

stephen_carpenter.name: Stephen Carpenter
stephen_carpenter.notes: An early adopter of extended-range guitars, moving from 6- to 7-, 8- and 9-string ESPs. Rack-based tone built on Fryette power amps, with a Marshall JMP-1 preamp in his rig.

trent_reznor.name: Trent Reznor
trent_reznor.notes: Recorded most guitars direct via a Marshall JMP-1 preamp into a Zoom 9030 speaker sim on The Downward Spiral. Heavy use of fuzz and a row of vintage pedals on The Fragile; later tours run a Kemper.

wes_borland.name: Wes Borland
wes_borland.notes: Used Mesa Dual/Triple Rectifiers across Limp Bizkit's first three albums with a Roland JC-120 for cleans, later moving to Orange Thunderverbs and EVH 5150 III heads. Known for theatrical, oddball gear.

dino_cazares.name: Dino Cazares
dino_cazares.notes: His early thrashy, scooped-mid chug came from a modified Marshall JCM800 with a noise gate (stolen in 1999). Pioneered 7- and 8-string baritone metal on Ibanez; later moved to Kemper and Ormsby guitars.

jim_root.name: Jim Root
jim_root.notes: Signature Fender Telecaster and Jazzmaster loaded with EMG 81/60 pickups, minimalist controls, through Orange Rockerverbs into Vintage 30 cabs. Plays in drop B with 11-56 strings.

mick_thomson.name: Mick Thomson
mick_thomson.notes: Drop-B rhythm machine running dual Rivera KR-7 heads with a RockCrusher load box, later Mesa JP-2C amps. Long associated with Ibanez MTM signatures before moving to an ESP LTD MT-I.

mark_tremonti.name: Mark Tremonti
mark_tremonti.notes: Built the Creed/Alter Bridge sound on PRS guitars and Mesa Rectifiers, later expanding to a multi-amp rig (Bogner Uberschall, Mesa Triples, Fender Twins for cleans) and his own PRS MT 100 signature amp.

robb_flynn.name: Robb Flynn
robb_flynn.notes: Groove-metal tone built on Peavey 5150 heads (he owns at least eight, his favorite named 'Bubba') into Marshall cabs, with an EH Electric Mistress flanger on every album. Plays EMG-loaded Epiphone and Washburn guitars.

devin_townsend.name: Devin Townsend
devin_townsend.notes: Wall-of-sound tone began with Peavey 5150s and Mesa Dual Rectifiers in the '90s, later fully embracing Fractal Audio. Plays in open tunings on his Framus Stormbender signature with Fishman pickups.

fredrik_thordendal.name: Fredrik Thordendal
fredrik_thordendal.notes: Polyrhythmic djent pioneer; pushed Ibanez to build the 8-string M8M signature. Meshuggah's whole guitar section moved to Fractal Audio Axe-Fx, later adding Fortin custom amps, with Lundgren M8 pickups.

wayne_static.name: Wayne Static
wayne_static.notes: Industrial-metal tone from a famously cheap Marshall MG100HDFX solid-state head, later going Line 6 POD XT Pro direct to PA. Played Seymour Duncan Distortion-loaded Epiphone and ESP signature V-shapes.

brian_head_welch.name: Brian "Head" Welch
brian_head_welch.notes: Co-architect of Korn's down-tuned 7-string sound, running Mesa Rectifiers (Dual then Triple Solo heads) into Mesa 4x12 cabs. Played Ibanez 7-strings through his first stint, later adding an ESP signature.

munky_shaffer.name: James "Munky" Shaffer
munky_shaffer.notes: Co-founder of Korn's 7-string nu-metal sound; used a 1990 Ibanez UV7BK on the debut. Early Korn ran Mesa Dual Rectos, later dual Triple Rectifier Solo heads and a Diezel VH4 into Mesa cabs.

michael_romeo.name: Michael Romeo
michael_romeo.notes: Neoclassical/prog-metal player running ENGL heads in stereo (Powerball, Fireball) with a TC G-System for time effects. Moved from ESP M-II to a Caparison Dellinger signature on Paradise Lost.

john_5.name: John 5
john_5.notes: Signature Fender Telecaster specialist (double-humbucker, country-meets-metal) running multiple Marshall JCM900 and JVM heads into 1960B cabs, with an EVH 5150. Joined Marilyn Manson in 1998, later Rob Zombie.

pat_obrien.name: Pat O'Brien
pat_obrien.notes: Death-metal precision built on Mesa Triple Rectifier heads (used on every Cannibal Corpse album since 1999's Bloodthirst) with EMG 81/85 pickups in Jackson V-shapes, later Ran signature guitars.

billie_joe_armstrong.name: Billie Joe Armstrong

guitars: Gibson Les Paul Junior Double Cut

billie_joe_armstrong.notes: Punk rock rhythm tone built on cranked Marshall Plexi heads (named 'Pete' and 'Meat') and a Seymour Duncan JB-loaded Fernandes Strat called 'Blue'. Defined the Dookie era.

noodles.name: Kevin "Noodles" Wasserman
noodles.notes: Punk lead tone from Mesa Boogie Mark IV amps (since ~1994) and light Ibanez Talman/signature NDM1 guitars run with the bridge pickup wide open.

tom_delonge.name: Tom DeLonge

amps: Fractal Audio Axe-FX III (Marshall Plexi + Soldano X88 models)

tom_delonge.notes: Single-pickup (Seymour Duncan Invader) guitars and Mesa Boogie Rectifier distortion define his thick pop-punk power-chord sound. Switched to Fractal modeling in 2022.

joe_bonamassa.name: Joe Bonamassa
joe_bonamassa.notes: Blues-rock revival tone from vintage Gibson Les Pauls into a multi-amp wall of Marshall, Fender Twin and Dumble heads. Career began ~2000-2001.

john_mayer.name: John Mayer
john_mayer.notes: Blues-pop tone from vintage and signature Stratocasters (later PRS Silver Sky) into Dumble, Two-Rock and PRS clean platforms. Debuted ~2001.

kenny_wayne_shepherd.name: Kenny Wayne Shepherd

guitars: Fender Kenny Wayne Shepherd Signature Stratocaster

kenny_wayne_shepherd.notes: Blues-revival Strat player whose '61 'Martha' Stratocaster runs into a collection of Dumble-modified Fender amps.

michael_weikath.name: Michael Weikath
michael_weikath.notes: Power-metal rhythm and lead tone from Gibson Flying V guitars into vintage Marshall and later ENGL heads. Co-founder of Helloween.

kai_hansen.name: Kai Hansen

guitars: ESP Custom V (Pink V, Bare Knuckle Juggernaut)

kai_hansen.notes: Speed/power-metal pioneer; his ESP custom Flying V models (notably the 'Pink V') run into Marshall and later ENGL high-gain heads. Founded Helloween and Gamma Ray.

andre_olbrich.name: André Olbrich
andre_olbrich.notes: Lead guitarist of power-metal band Blind Guardian, known for layered melodic leads through ESP Horizon customs and ENGL Straight heads (later Fractal Axe-FX).

timo_tolkki.name: Timo Tolkki
timo_tolkki.notes: Neoclassical power-metal guitarist and main songwriter of Stratovarius; played ESP M-II guitars through Mesa Boogie and ENGL high-gain amps.

greg_hetson.name: Greg Hetson
greg_hetson.notes: Punk rhythm/lead tone from Gibson SG guitars into a Marshall JCM800 and Mesa Boogie Dual Rectifier. Longtime Bad Religion and Circle Jerks guitarist.

brian_baker.name: Brian Baker
brian_baker.notes: Joined Bad Religion in 1994; his 1955 Gibson Les Paul Junior with a dog-ear P-90 runs into Dookie-modded Marshall 1987X plexi heads.

flea.name: Flea
flea.notes: Funk-rock slap and fingerstyle tone; moved from Music Man StingRay to his signature Modulus Flea Bass, powered by Gallien-Krueger rigs (later Ampeg SVT).

les_claypool.name: Les Claypool
les_claypool.notes: Virtuoso slap/tap and pick bassist of Primus; built his sound on EMG-loaded fretless Carl Thompson basses (notably the 'Rainbow Bass') into Mesa Boogie rigs.

jason_newsted.name: Jason Newsted
jason_newsted.notes: Metallica bassist 1986-2001; aggressive picked metal tone from P/J-style basses (Sadowsky, Spector, Alembic) into Ampeg SVT rigs, often with a Trace Elliot preamp and SansAmp.

mike_dirnt.name: Mike Dirnt

guitars: Fender Mike Dirnt Signature Precision Bass

mike_dirnt.notes: Punk bassist of Green Day; driving pick-style Precision Bass tone (his signature P-Bass) through Ampeg SVT and later Fender Super Bassman heads.

mark_hoppus.name: Mark Hoppus

guitars: Fender Mark Hoppus Signature Bass (Jazz body, P pickup)

mark_hoppus.notes: Pop-punk bassist of Blink-182; punchy pick tone from his signature Jazz-body/P-pickup bass into Ampeg SVT rigs, later switching to Kemper/Quad Cortex modeling.

justin_chancellor.name: Justin Chancellor
justin_chancellor.notes: Tool bassist since 1995; his signature growling, distorted tone comes from Wal 4-string basses split into clean and dirty Mesa Boogie/Gallien-Krueger amp paths.

paul_damour.name: Paul D'Amour
paul_damour.notes: Original Tool bassist (Undertow era); aggressive picked tone from a Rickenbacker 4001CS into a Mesa Boogie Bass 400+ rig before leaving in 1995.

rex_brown.name: Rex Brown
rex_brown.notes: Pantera bassist; classic groove-metal low end from Spector NS-2 basses into Ampeg SVT preamps and 8x10 cabs, often via a SansAmp DI.

fat_mike.name: Fat Mike
fat_mike.notes: Punk bassist and frontman of NOFX; bright, driving pick-style tone from Fender P-Basses (and a Danelectro DC) into Fender Super Bassman/Ampeg rigs.

dirk_lance.name: Dirk Lance
dirk_lance.notes: Original Incubus bassist (Alex Katunich); warm, funky fingerstyle tone from active Warwick Streamer Stage II basses into a tri-amped Eden rig.
